---
title: MoTrPAC
alias: &ALIAS motrpac
href: https://motrpac-data.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Molecular Transducers of Physical Activity Consortium (MoTrPAC) is an NIH Common Fund program generating comprehensive molecular datasets to understand how physical activity improves and maintains health.

## Key Features

- Generates multi-omic data (transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics, epigenomics) from exercise studies
- Covers multiple tissues and time points in both human clinical trials and rat animal models
- Provides a public data hub with standardized processed data and analysis pipelines
- Employs rigorous QC and harmonized protocols across multiple clinical and animal sites
- Supports systems-level analysis of exercise-induced molecular adaptations

## Use Cases in Research

- Investigating how physical activity modulates immune function and susceptibility to infection
- Analyzing exercise-induced changes in gut microbiome composition and metabolic outputs
- Using multi-omic datasets to develop AI models linking lifestyle factors to health outcomes
- Identifying molecular targets altered by exercise that intersect with AMR-relevant pathways
- Benchmarking multi-omic data integration methods against a well-characterized intervention dataset

## Getting Started

Visit [MoTrPAC](https://motrpac-data.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
